req.title: Procedure record
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-011 mandates that a Procedure resource can be created only when tied to a valid Encounter. Three core partitions emerge: (1) valid encounter reference yields 201 Created with stored procedure (EP/positive), (2) missing encounter reference yields 422 Unprocessable Entity (NEG/negative), and (3) encounter reference present but the encounter is in a terminal/closed state tests the boundary of the 'open or referenced' precondition (BVA/boundary). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down their own resources, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A valid Encounter resource exists with status 'in-progress'
actions: POST /Procedure with a well-formed FHIR Procedure body referencing the open Encounter ID
Capture the HTTP response status and Location header
expected: Response body encounter.reference matches the created Encounter ID
A Location header pointing to the new Procedure resource is present
trace: R-HLT-011: procedure stored when encounter open or referenced
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201(
    base_url, auth_headers, create_encounter, cleanup_resource
):
    encounter_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress")
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                         "code": "80146002",
                         "display": "Appendectomy"}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Procedure"
    assert body["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{encounter_id}"
    assert "Location" in resp.headers
    cleanup_resource("Procedure", body["id"])
    cleanup_resource("Encounter", encounter_id)

tests.name: test_create_procedure_without_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
No encounter reference is included in the request payload
actions: POST /Procedure with a Procedure body that omits the encounter field entirely
Capture the HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message indicating missing encounter
trace: R-HLT-011: error pathway 422 when encounter missing

tests.name: test_create_procedure_referencing_finished_encounter_boundary
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
An Encounter resource exists with status 'finished' (terminal state boundary)
actions: POST /Procedure referencing the finished Encounter ID
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP status is either 201 (system allows retrospective recording) or 422 (system enforces open-only constraint)
If 201: response body encounter.reference matches the finished Encounter ID
If 422: response body contains an OperationOutcome or error indicating encounter state violation
trace: R-HLT-011: precondition 'encounter open or referenced' — boundary between open and closed encounter states
R-HLT-011: BVA on encounter status transition finished/in-progress

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_nonexistent_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Encounter ID used does not exist in the system
actions: POST /Procedure referencing a syntactically valid but non-existent Encounter ID
Capture the HTTP response status and error body
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity or 404 Not Found
Response body indicates the referenced Encounter could not be resolved
trace: R-HLT-011: error pathway 422 when encounter missing — covers phantom reference variant
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_with_nonexistent_encounter_returns_422(
    base_url, auth_headers
):
    phantom_id = "Encounter/nonexistent-id-000000"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": phantom_id},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                         "code": "80146002",
                         "display": "Appendectomy"}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (422, 404)
    body = resp.json()
    assert (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "encounter" in str(body).lower()
        or "not found" in str(body).lower()
    )

tests.name: test_procedure_persisted_and_retrievable_after_post
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A valid Encounter resource exists with status 'in-progress'
actions: POST /Procedure with a valid body referencing the open Encounter
Extract the new Procedure ID from the 201 response
GET /Procedure/{id} to verify the resource is stored and retrievable
expected: GET response body encounter.reference matches the original Encounter ID
trace: R-HLT-011: expected outcome 'procedure stored' — verified via read-back
R-HLT-011: STC transitions: not-exist -> created -> retrievable
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_procedure_persisted_and_retrievable_after_post(
    base_url, auth_headers, create_encounter, cleanup_resource
):
    encounter_id = create_encounter(status="in-progress")
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                         "code": "173171007",
                         "display": "Lobectomy"}]
        }
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    proc_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure/{proc_id}", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Procedure"
    assert body["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{encounter_id}"
    cleanup_resource("Procedure", proc_id)
    cleanup_resource("Encounter", encounter_id)
